name: Mughal Empire (Alam, 1526–1857)

colors.symbolism: Mughal green of the imperial standard

description: The imperial Alam: a moss-green field bearing a gold lion and a rising sun (Sher-o-khurshid) facing the hoist.
history: The Alam was the principal imperial standard of the Mughal Empire. The lion-and-sun motif, traced by the Mughals to Timurid heritage, is documented from the reign of Akbar in the Ain-i-Akbari and was displayed beside the throne and carried before the emperor in battle and on festive days.
